page: OVHcloud rtx5000-84 is a GPU-accelerated server instance built on the KVM hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon W-3235 processor with 16 dedicated physical cores operating at a base frequency of 3.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 84.0 GB of system memory, providing 5.25 GB per core. For accelerated workloads, the server integrates three NVIDIA Quadro RTX 5000 GPUs based on the Turing architecture, delivering a combined total of 48 GB of VRAM. Storage is provided via a 400 GB local SSD, and network connectivity is supported by a 10 Gbps baseline bandwidth.

Benchmark data indicates strong performance in memory-bound and parallel processing workloads. The instance achieved a Passmark memory score categorized as strong, with read-only small block memory bandwidth reaching 990.44 GB/sec. CPU performance is stable, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 8459.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 20742.83. Notably, the server excels in LLM inference benchmarks, placing in the top tier for prompt processing and text generation across small, medium, and large models. However, single-threaded text processing represents a relative bottleneck, scoring in the bottom tier on Geekbench.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the rtx5000-84 offers a dense hardware profile by bundling three dedicated GPUs with 16 physical CPU cores and local SSD storage, making it a viable option for multi-GPU workloads without the premium of top-tier enterprise accelerators. The instance is highly suited for workloads such as large language model inference, parallel data processing, ray tracing, and machine learning tasks that benefit from distributed GPU memory.
description: The OVHcloud rtx5000-84 is a GPU-accelerated server instance featuring an Intel Xeon W-3235 processor with 16 dedicated physical cores running at 3.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. It is equipped with 84.0 GB of RAM, a 400 GB local SSD, and 10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. The core highlight is its triple NVIDIA Quadro RTX 5000 GPU configuration, providing a total of 48 GB of VRAM. Benchmark results show average CPU and memory performance, but top-tier capabilities in LLM inference for prompt processing and text generation across various model sizes. While single-threaded text processing is a relative weakness, the instance delivers strong memory bandwidth and ray tracing performance. This hardware profile is highly suited for GPU-intensive workloads, including machine learning inference, data science, and parallel rendering tasks.
og_description: OVHcloud rtx5000-84 is a GPU-accelerated server featuring 16 dedicated Intel Xeon cores, 84 GB RAM, a 400 GB SSD, and three NVIDIA Quadro RTX 5000 GPUs providing 48 GB of total VRAM.
meta_description: OVHcloud rtx5000-84 is a GPU-accelerated server with 16 Intel Xeon cores, 84 GB RAM, and three NVIDIA RTX 5000 GPUs for parallel computing.
tagline: A dedicated multi-GPU server featuring three Turing-architecture graphics cards and sixteen physical cores for parallel computing and model inference.
bullet_points: Three NVIDIA Quadro RTX 5000 GPUs providing 48 GB of total VRAM
Intel Xeon W-3235 processor with 16 dedicated physical cores at 3.3 GHz
84 GB of system memory and 400 GB of local SSD storage
Top-tier performance in LLM inference for small, medium, and large models
10 Gbps baseline network bandwidth with one public IPv4 address
Best suited for machine learning inference, ray tracing, and parallel data processing
